exemplar.title: Which rhetorical devices in Malala Yousafzaiâs speeches create the most impact on her audience to inspire change in patriarchal societies?

detailedExemplarData.title: Which rhetorical devices in Malala Yousafzaiâs speeches create the most impact on her audience to inspire change in patriarchal societies?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Succinct introduction establishing context and purpose
Relevant quotations from four speeches with mostly correct referencing
Clear linkage of devices to the central research question
Generally coherent interpretation of implications related to patriarchy challenge
weaknesses: Contextual background lacks depth on Taliban socio-politics
Interpretation is uneven and sometimes speculative
Some citations have minor inaccuracies in dates and formatting
Technical terminology occasionally undefined or used mechanically
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the work aligns with Band 3 for knowledge in some respects, its consistent identification of key moments across four speeches, generally effective referencing and sustained focus on the research question push it into the lower end of Band 4. The student demonstrates âgood knowledge and understanding with sustained interpretationââhence a score of 4.

strengths: Clear identification of three repetition devices (antimetabole, anaphora, diacope)
Appropriate linking of each device to possible audience effects
Occasional insightful observations (e.g., pronoun reversal reciprocity)
Sound use of terminology to frame analysis
weaknesses: Analysis often drifts into description rather than probing linguistic mechanisms
Conceptual overlap between devices (irony vs. metonymy vs. diacope)
Lack of textual markers or quotations to substantiate claims about pace
Evaluative comments remain surface-level without sustained depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay offers generally appropriate analysis and at times insightful evaluationâmeeting the Band 4 descriptor of âgenerally appropriate analysis and evaluation of textual features and authorâs choices.â While not consistently deep, its strengths outweigh the weaknesses, justifying a 4.

strengths: Well-defined introductionâbodyâconclusion structure tied to the research question
Logical progression with clear topic sentences for each device
Mostly cohesive argument with synthesis in the conclusion
Effective use of connective phrases to guide the reader
weaknesses: Integration of long quotations is sometimes awkward or mid-sentence
Transitions can be repetitive and some paragraphs digress into side points
Final synthesis could more directly restate how each device answers the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization is solid and the line of inquiry is well developed (Band 4), though integration of examples occasionally falters (Band 3). Overall, the cohesion and development merit a 4, reflecting âwell organized and mostly cohesiveâ writing.

strengths: Language is generally clear, with appropriate academic vocabulary
Consistent third-person register and formal style
Sentence structures mostly competent, facilitating reader comprehension
Correct use of rhetorical terminology in context
weaknesses: Noticeable grammatical lapses (tense shifts, missing articles)
Awkward phrasing in places and occasional run-on sentences
Typographical errors and inconsistent MLA formatting
Some informality (âmove to get educationâ) disrupts precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite errors, the essayâs clarity and overall accuracy exceed the Band 3 descriptor and align with Band 4: âlanguage clear with adequate accuracy in grammar, vocabulary and sentence construction despite some lapses.â Therefore, a score of 4 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Supporting quotations are placed within paragraphs, but integration is inconsistent: several lengthy passages are inserted with minimal syntactic blending, and explanatory sentences sometimes follow rather than embed the evidence. Still, the student does attempt to weave commentary around most citations and avoids simple bullet-point listing. 

Given this mixture of adequate and awkward integration, the work best matches Band 3: âsupporting examples sometimes integrated into sentences and paragraphs.â

The student demonstrates **satisfactory contextual knowledge** of Malala Yousafzaiâs speeches, correctly outlining her background, occasions of delivery and overall purpose. Key moments from four different speeches are named, showing a solid awareness of where and why the lines were spoken. Interpretation of implications â such as how inclusive pronouns place responsibility on the audience â is present but remains general and occasionally speculative, lacking the sustained, nuanced discussion characteristic of the higher bands. 

While the essay keeps the central research question in view and links devices to the aim of challenging patriarchy, the depth of discussion is uneven; historical context and ideological significance are touched on only briefly. Consequently the work fits the âsatisfactory knowledge and understanding with interpretation of implications related to the topicâ descriptor (Band 3).

The essay follows a clear introductionâbodyâconclusion structure anchored to the research question, and each body section focuses on a single rhetorical device. Topic sentences, connective phrases (âAnother exampleâ¦â, âThe last techniqueâ¦â) and a concluding synthesis paragraph create **mostly cohesive progression**. 

While transitions can be repetitive and some paragraphs contain digressions, the overall line of inquiry is identifiable and sufficiently developed, meeting the âwell organized and mostly cohesiveâ description in Band 4.

Quotations from each speech are supplied and mostly relevant; line numbers or source titles are offered, and an MLA Works-Cited list is appended, indicating clear effort to reference material. These references **generally support** the studentâs points, for example the repeated citation of âOne child, one teacherâ¦â when analysing antimetabole. 

However, quotation integration is sometimes mechanical (e.g., block quotes dropped mid-sentence) and the analysis often reiterates rather than extends the evidence. Some claims (such as accelerated pace demonstrating heartbreak) are asserted without textual proof. This mixture of useful but inconsistently exploited evidence aligns with Band 3: âreferences are generally relevant and mostly support the candidateâs ideas.â

The student maintains an **academic, third-person style** for most of the essay, employs formal vocabulary, and avoids colloquial language, satisfying basic expectations of register. Occasional informality or rhetorical flourish (âmakes her heartbroken,â âtake many actionsâ) and direct address to âdear friendsâ within quotations slightly disrupt consistency but do not undermine overall appropriateness. 

Consequently, the work aligns with Band 3: âregister and style mostly appropriate to the task,â rather than the more polished consistency required for Band 4.

Language is **largely clear and understandable**, enabling communication of the argument. Vocabulary is appropriate to an academic register (âantimetabole,â âpatriarchal norms,â âmetonymyâ), and sentence structure is generally competent. 

However, noticeable lapsesâtense shifts, missing articles, awkward phrasing (âher pace quicken,â âmove to get educationâ)âoccur throughout, preventing the higher accuracy demanded by Band 4. Thus Band 3, which allows for âsome lapses,â is the most fitting placement.

The student identifies three figures of repetitionâantimetabole, anaphora and diacopeâand offers **generally appropriate explanations** of how each may affect an audience. For instance, the discussion of pronoun reversal in the Oxford speech shows awareness of structural patterning, and the section on diacope links to irony and metonymy. 

Nevertheless, much commentary drifts into description or impact assertions (âmakes the message memorableâ) without probing HOW linguistic choices create those effects; stylistic terminology beyond the three devices is limited, and evaluative remarks are surface-level. Occasional insight appears but is not sustained, placing the work in Band 3 rather than the more incisive Band 4.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated and establishes a precise focus on examining rhetorical strategies in Malalaâs speeches.
Consider refining the question to specify the desired persuasive effects on the audience (e.g., emotional response or call to action) to narrow the inquiry further.
The introduction succinctly presents Malalaâs background, Nobel accolade, and overarching purpose in advocating girlsâ education.
The phrase âto silence her when she was 15 due to advocacy for girl's education in publicâ is awkwardly constructed. Revise for grammatical clarity and precision.
Deepen the contextual background by discussing the socio-political dynamics of Taliban rule to enhance your interpretation of her rhetorical choices.
Explicitly state in your thesis how each device (antimetabole, anaphora, diacope) will contribute to persuading patriarchal audiences.
Good selection of the âOne child, one teacher, one pen and one bookâ antimetabole example illustrates her singular-to-plural inversion.
Quotation is inserted mid-sentence without syntactic integration. Embed it fully into your own phrasing to improve cohesion.
Expand your analysis by showing how antimetabole amplifies ethos or pathos among audience members in patriarchal contexts.
Typographical error: âfeatureâ should read âfuture.â Correct this to avoid distracting readers.
Link diacopeâs repetition effect more explicitly to audience emotion by discussing how recurrence fosters urgency.
Paragraphs on page 5 conflate multiple ideas without clear transitions, which obscures the progression of your argument.
The exploration of antithesis and metonymy would benefit from deeper analysis of how cognitive dissonance influences audience receptivity.
This concluding sentence is a run-on; breaking it into shorter, clearer sentences will improve readability and impact.
Reorient the final sentence to directly answer the research question, reinforcing how repetition devices inspire change.
Ensure consistent MLA formatting by italicizing website titles and standardizing accessâdate punctuation across all entries.
Insightful identification of the pronoun reversal (âI will see themâ to âthey will see meâ) highlighting reciprocal agency.
The phrase âmove to get educationâ is informal and imprecise; use academic vocabulary to articulate this idea more effectively.
Provide concrete textual cues (e.g., shorter sentences, punctuation) to substantiate your claim about pace changes during delivery.
Accurate identification of anaphora in the repeated âdear friends,â âdear brothers and sisters,â and âdear fellows.â
Discussion of memorability via repetition is descriptive; analyze how cognitive processing of repeated phrases emotionally engages the audience.
Clarify the connection between anaphora and pathos by detailing the emotional resonance with specific audience segments.
Claims about pace quickening and emotional state require supporting evidenceâquote markers or notation indicating delivery speed.
The analysis conflates irony and metonymy with diacope, leading to conceptual overlap. Separate each device discussion for clarity.
Include a concise definition of diacope (repetition of a word or phrase with intervening text) to strengthen your technical terminology.
Well-chosen diacope example from the 16th birthday speech demonstrates her insistence on memory and collective responsibility.
Verify the publication date in this citation (â11 Apr. 2022â) against the actual speech date for accuracy in your Works Cited.
The comprehensive list of sources demonstrates thorough research across multiple platforms, strengthening credibility.
